title: <name> and the Secret of the Singing Banyan

pages.text: It was a warm, breezy afternoon when <name> wandered near the old banyan tree at the edge of his village. Its thick roots twisted and curled like the arms of gentle giants. As the wind rustled through the leaves, a soft, musical hum seemed to rise from the roots. <name> crouched down and noticed something incredible — a tiny door hidden among the roots, glowing faintly in the sunlight.

Curious, he pressed his hand to it. The door creaked open as the wind sang through the roots, revealing a dim path lined with glowing fireflies. They fluttered like tiny lanterns, guiding him deeper into a secret garden that no one in the village had ever seen.

The path led to a sparkling pond surrounded by smooth stepping stones. A riddle appeared etched on a stone beside the water in Hindi: "जो प्रश्न सोचता है, वही उत्तर पाता है।" ("The one who thinks finds the answer.")

<name> thought carefully, remembering what his grandfather had said about listening to nature. He stepped lightly across the stones, one by one, following the fireflies, until he reached the pond. At the center, ancient carvings shimmered in the moonlight, showing scenes of villagers planting trees, sharing water, and caring for animals.

Suddenly, a gentle breeze whispered through the banyan leaves. <name> realized the tree had been protecting the village all along, keeping its secrets safe for those who were kind and curious. Smiling, he promised to always respect nature, knowing that courage was not just about bravery, but also about care and curiosity.
With that, <name> returned to the village, the singing banyan waving its branches as if saying goodbye — a secret friend he would never forget.
